title: Event Horizon Telescope (VLBI) Imaging
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Event Horizon Telescope (VLBI) Imaging: VLBI imaging: recover brightness distribution from sparse Fourier samples (visibilities) with closure-phase constraints. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG (F.fourier.nufft…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~1000; station_gain_error, atmospheric_delay_error set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: V(u,v) = FT[I(l,m)](u,v) + noise; closure phases C_ijk = arg(V_ij * V_jk * V_ki*) noise-robust
E.world_state_x: brightness temperature image I(l,m) in R^{H x W}, total flux conserved
E.observation_y: complex visibilities V(u,v) at N_baselines sparse (u,v) locations; closure phases

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D_brightness_temperature is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 1000); station_gain_error dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Thermal gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: chi2_reduced ~ 1.0 at convergence; dynamic range >= 100:1. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~1000. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |station_gain_error|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_baselines * N_pixels * N_iter) for gradient-based imager per iteration
